characters.name: Bonnie Brindle
characters.description: Bonnie Bubbly and mawkishly demure Bonnie Brindle (played by Edie McClurg) is the second thorn in the Lawsons' flesh, being Brandon's bossy wife and the source of Harriet's snooping gifts and busybody attitude. Bonnie is renowned for her classic "No no-no No no-no!!" whenever reproving some sensible situation. It is because of Bonnie's meddling that the Lawsons are forced to "adopt" Vicki or lose her to the state as a lost waif. A member of the bawdy "Gutter Gals" bowling team, Bonnie shows no sign of independent support and seems to make a career of being the local National Enquirer. First appearance: S1E2 - The Neighbors

characters.name: Brandon Brindle
characters.description: Brandon Brindleis a character who appeared in the second episode in the first season of Small Wonder. He is a co-worker ofTed Lawsonat United Robotronics and his next door neighbor as well. He has a wife,Bonnie Brindleand a daughter namedHarrietwho's very nosy around the Lawson's especiallyJamie. First appearance:The Neighbors, episode 2, season 1. Episode appearances:

characters.name: Grandpa "Bill" Lawson
characters.description: Grandpa "Bill" Lawsonis a character that appeared in the final episode in the first season of Small Wonder.

characters.name: Harriet Brindle
characters.description: Harriet Brindle(played byEmily Schulman) is character who appeared in the 1980's sitcom,Small Wonder. She's the nosy neighbor who always wondered what the Lawson's were up to, especially their son,Jamiewho she has a crush on and always dreamed of marrying him. First appearance: "Vicki's Homecoming", episode 1, season 1.

characters.name: Jamie Lawson
characters.description: Jamie Lawson(Jerry Supiran) is a character inSmall Wonder. He's the son ofTed Lawson, the engineer who built V.I.C.I. (Voice Input Child Identicant) a.k.a.Vickithe Robot, andJoan Lawson. Born:March 21, 1973

characters.name: Jesse "the Body" Ventura
characters.description: Jesse "the Body" Ventura(himself) is aprofessional wrestlerwho made a guess appearance in a episode of Small Wonder. He appeared in the episode, "Wally the wimp."

characters.name: Joan Lawson
characters.description: Joan Lawson(played byMarla Pennington) is a character inSmall Wonder. She's the wife of the United Robotronics engineer,Ted Lawson, who constructed the V.I.C.I. (Voice Input Child Identicant) a.k.a.Vickithe Robot and the mother ofJamie Lawsonwho at times likes to refer to himself as "the Big J".

characters.name: Rodney the Robot
characters.description: Rodney the Robotwas a character that appeared in the second episode of Small Wonder in the first season. First appearance:The Neighbors, episode 2, season 1.

characters.name: Home
characters.description: The main goal of the Small Wonder wiki is to preserve the history of the 1980s TV seriesSmall Wonderfor future generations.  Photos and videos are a great way to add visuals to your wiki. Find videos about your topic by exploring Wikia'sVideo Library.

characters.name: Home
characters.description: The main goal of the Small Wonder wiki is to preserve the history of the 1980s TV seriesSmall Wonderfor future generations.  Photos and videos are a great way to add visuals to your wiki. Find videos about your topic by exploring Wikia'sVideo Library.

characters.name: Ted Lawson
characters.description: Ted Lawson(played byRichard Christie) is a character inSmall Wonder. He works as an engineer and is the creator of V.I.C.I (Voice Input Child Identicant) a.k.a.Vickithe Robot. He has a wife,Joan Lawsonand a son namedJamie. Although he's the one who constructed Vicki, he also accepts her as his daughter. When he isn't working at United Robotronics or isn't working with Vicki at home, he'll play a game of golf. First appearance: S1E1 - Vicki's Homecoming

characters.name: Vanessa
characters.description: Vanessa Vanessa Lawson(Tiffany Brissette) is Vicki's more advanced twin sister and "Bad Seed" flip side, whose revolutionary but arbitrary A.I. shows up in her arrogance, impulsiveness and conceit. With a sniffy normal human voice given by a true vocal tract, Vanessa, unlike docile Vicki, possesses a naughty and impetuous "persona" because her PEP was expanded and fine-tuned to mimic the behavior of real girls via digitized live-model sampling. She has a limited ability to judge and make decisions and even reason after a fashion and have a sense of aesthetics such as loving rock n' roll and criticizing kitchen decor and styles. Unfortunately, because digitizing the social behavior of human girls is a lot easier than digitizing a conscious and ethics, her ungoverned PEP also sports an excessively vain and lethal ego. Sly and deviously demure to dominate family and humans, she contemptuously looks upon Vicki as a "dummy" and wonders whether the only worth of human beings is that they might taste good. Because of Vanessa's formidable speed and strength, the Lawsons are only able to subdue her by sheer trickery. Vanessa acts more like a rebel without a cause than a renegade juvenile delinquent and this is likely attributable to Ted's underestimating her wayward restless potential as a simple "character flaw" by relegating her to household tasks which are "beneath her". Though Vanessa appears only twice, she was very popular and the seed for a potential spin-off. Trivia: She Shares One Thing with Vicki They Both Have an Emergency Off Switch on Top of Their Heads,

characters.name: Vicki
characters.description: Tiffany Brissette as Vicki. Victoria "Vicki" Ann Smith-Lawson(Tiffany Brissette) - A robot modeled after a real human girl. Her name is an abbreviation of Voice Input Child Identicant (V.I.C.I.). Vicki has real hair and realistic skin. She possesses superhuman strength and speed and runs on atomic power. Vicki has an access panel in her back, an electric socket in her right armpit, and an RS-232serial portunder her left armpit. Vicki's artificial intelligence is not perfect. She is incapable ofemotion, speaks in a monotone voice, and interprets most commands literally. She does manage to blend into the real world to a point. Vicki attends school, and no one but her family members and a few trusted friends know her secret. Occasionally Vicki had rare abilities that seemed to only appear in one or two episodes, such as elongating her neck to reach a door's peephole, shrinking her size to become as small as a doll or making herself ten feet tall to get noticed by everyone. One recurring theme was that Vicki had a super-powered learning system which enabled her to improve upon something such as a new detergent or to greatly increase the gas mileage of cars, whichTedandJamieoften saw as a chance to get rich quick, only to find her improvements were not perfect. Vicki lives in a large cabinet in Jamie's bedroom and becomes more human over the course of the show.
